seoTitle: How to Generate an Expense Report by Customer in QuickBooks
metaDescription: Break down business expenses by customer in QuickBooks Online to see spending patterns and make more informed budgeting decisions.
intro: Seeing which customers or projects incur the most expenses helps with budgeting and resource allocation, and QuickBooks Online makes it possible to generate that breakdown directly. This guide covers building an expense report by customer and using it for financial decisions.

steps.heading: Navigate to the reports section
steps.body: Open the reporting area within QuickBooks Online where expense reports can be generated.

steps.heading: Select the expense report by customer option
steps.body: Choose the report type that breaks down expenses specifically by customer rather than overall totals.

steps.heading: Set the date range and filters
steps.body: Adjust the report's time period and any relevant filters to focus on the data you need.

steps.heading: Run and review the report
steps.body: Generate the report and review how expenses are distributed across different customers.

steps.heading: Use the insights for decision-making
steps.body: Apply the findings to budgeting, pricing decisions, or client relationship management as needed.

faq.question: Who benefits most from an expense report broken down by customer?
faq.answer: Freelancers tracking client-specific costs and small business owners managing multiple accounts both benefit from this level of detail.

faq.question: Can I automate this report to run regularly?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online offers scheduling and automation options for many reports, which can save time on recurring reviews.

faq.question: What insights can this report reveal?
faq.answer: It can highlight which customers or projects are the most expensive to serve, helping inform pricing or resource allocation decisions.

faq.question: Can I integrate this report with other financial tools?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online supports integrations with various software tools that can pull in report data for broader financial analysis.
